segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: How did Sam get his money back?

translations.zh-Hans: 萨姆是怎样拿回自己的钱的？

segments.text: The whole village soon learnt that a large sum of money had been lost.

translations.zh-Hans: 全村很快都知道有一大笔钱丢失了。

segments.text: Sam Benton, the local butcher, had lost his wallet while taking his savings to the post office.

translations.zh-Hans: 当地的肉商萨姆·本顿在把积蓄送往邮局时丢了钱包。

segments.text: Sam was sure that the wallet must have been found by one of the villagers, but it was not returned to him.

translations.zh-Hans: 萨姆确信钱包一定被某个村民捡到了，但没有人把它还给他。

segments.text: Three months passed, and then one morning Sam found his wallet outside his front door.

translations.zh-Hans: 三个月过去了。一天早晨，萨姆在自家前门外发现了钱包。

segments.text: It had been wrapped up in newspaper, and it contained half the money he had lost, together with a note which said, A thief, yes, but only fifty percent a thief.

translations.zh-Hans: 钱包用报纸包着，里面装着他丢失的一半钱，还有一张纸条，上面写着：“是小偷，但只是个百分之五十的小偷。”

segments.text: Two months later, some more money was sent to Sam with another note.

translations.zh-Hans: 两个月后，又有一些钱连同另一张纸条寄给了萨姆。

segments.text: Only twenty-five percent a thief now.

translations.zh-Hans: “现在只是个百分之二十五的小偷了。”

segments.text: In time, all Sam's money was paid back in this way.

translations.zh-Hans: 最终，萨姆所有的钱都以这种方式还了回来。

segments.text: The last note said, I am one hundred percent honest now.

translations.zh-Hans: 最后一张纸条写着：“我现在百分之百诚实了。”
